---
title: "{{TITLE}}"
subtitle: "Systematic review protocol"
keywords:
  - systematic review
  - protocol
  - PRISMA-P
abstract: |
  **Background:** State the rationale for the review and the gap in
  current knowledge it addresses.

  **Methods:** Eligibility criteria framed using PICO (Population,
  Intervention, Comparator, Outcomes), information sources, search
  strategy, study selection, data extraction, risk of bias assessment,
  and synthesis approach.

  **Discussion:** Anticipated contribution and limitations.

  **Registration:** PROSPERO registration: <PROSPERO ID>

  **Keywords:** systematic review; protocol; PRISMA-P; (replace with
  topic-specific terms)

version-history:
  - version: "0.1.0"
    date: "{{DATE}}"
    description: "Initial draft"
---

::: version-history
:::

::: author-plate
:::

::: toc
:::

# Article summary

## Strengths and limitations

- Strength: PRISMA-P-aligned protocol pre-registered on PROSPERO before
  screening begins.
- Strength: a-priori-defined PICO, eligibility criteria, search
  strategy, and risk-of-bias assessment plan.
- Limitation: (review-specific — e.g. heterogeneity expected, language
  restrictions, study-design limitations)
- Limitation: (add as needed)

# 1. Introduction

## 1.1 Rationale

Describe the rationale for the review in the context of what is already
known. Reference key prior systematic reviews and their findings,
limitations, or contradictions.

## 1.2 Objectives

The primary objective is to (verb) the (outcome) of (intervention) for
(population) in (context).

### 1.2.1 PICO

- **Population:** (who)
- **Intervention:** (what is being evaluated)
- **Comparator:** (against what; or "any" / "none")
- **Outcomes:** (primary and secondary outcomes, with measurement
  definitions)

# 2. Methods

## 2.1 Eligibility criteria

### 2.1.1 Study designs

- (Specify designs to include: RCTs only, RCTs + cohort, etc.)
- (Specify designs to exclude)

### 2.1.2 Participants

- (Population characteristics, age range, condition, severity, etc.)

### 2.1.3 Interventions

- (Intervention specification: dose, duration, delivery context)

### 2.1.4 Comparators

- (Comparator specification, if any)

### 2.1.5 Outcomes

**Primary outcomes:**

- (Primary outcome 1 with measurement definition)

**Secondary outcomes:**

- (Secondary outcomes with measurement definitions)

### 2.1.6 Other criteria

- Language: (specify)
- Publication date: (specify range)
- Study setting: (specify if applicable)

## 2.2 Information sources

Databases to be searched:

- MEDLINE (Ovid)
- Embase (Ovid)
- Cochrane Central Register of Controlled Trials (CENTRAL)
- (Add or remove as appropriate)

Trial registries:

- ClinicalTrials.gov
- WHO ICTRP
- (Add as appropriate)

Grey literature: (specify sources)

Reference lists of included studies and relevant systematic reviews
will be screened for additional eligible studies.

## 2.3 Search strategy

The search strategy was developed with an information specialist and
peer-reviewed using the PRESS checklist [@mcgowan2016]. The full
strategy is in `supplements/search-strategy.json`.

## 2.4 Study records

### 2.4.1 Data management

Records will be deduplicated using (Zotero / EndNote / Covidence) and
managed in (Covidence / Rayyan / DistillerSR).

### 2.4.2 Selection process

Two reviewers will independently screen titles and abstracts against
the eligibility criteria. Disagreements will be resolved by discussion
or by a third reviewer. Studies appearing potentially eligible will be
retrieved in full and assessed independently by two reviewers.

A PRISMA flow diagram will report the number of records at each stage.

### 2.4.3 Data collection process

A standardised data extraction form will be piloted on a calibration
sample (5 studies) before full extraction. Two reviewers will extract
data independently; disagreements will be resolved by discussion or by
a third reviewer.

## 2.5 Data items

The data extraction form (`supplements/data-extraction-fields.csv`)
will capture, at minimum:

- Bibliographic data (citation, year, country)
- Study design and setting
- Participant characteristics (age, sex, condition severity)
- Intervention details (dose, duration, delivery)
- Comparator details
- Outcome data (estimates, measures of variability, sample sizes)
- Funding source and conflicts of interest

## 2.6 Outcomes and prioritisation

Outcomes are listed in section 2.1.5 above. The primary outcome will
drive the main synthesis; secondary outcomes will be reported but
interpreted with appropriate caution given multiple-comparison concerns.

## 2.7 Risk of bias assessment

Risk of bias will be assessed using:

- (Specify tool: Cochrane RoB 2 for RCTs; ROBINS-I for non-randomised
  studies; QUADAS-2 for diagnostic accuracy; etc.)

Two reviewers will assess risk of bias independently; disagreements
will be resolved by discussion or by a third reviewer.

## 2.8 Data synthesis

(Specify: meta-analysis if appropriate, narrative synthesis otherwise.
If meta-analysis: specify model — fixed/random effects — and
heterogeneity assessment using I² and τ². If subgroup analyses or
sensitivity analyses are planned, specify them now.)

If quantitative synthesis is not feasible, results will be reported
narratively, structured by outcome.

## 2.9 Meta-bias assessment

Publication bias will be assessed using funnel plots and Egger's test
if at least 10 studies report the primary outcome. Selective outcome
reporting will be assessed by comparing reported outcomes against trial
registration entries.

## 2.10 Confidence in cumulative evidence

The certainty of evidence for each outcome will be rated using GRADE
[@guyatt2008].

# 3. Discussion

(Anticipated contribution and limitations of the review approach.)

# 4. Ethics

This review uses only published secondary literature. Ethics committee
approval is not required.

# 5. Registration

The protocol is registered on PROSPERO: <PROSPERO ID>.

# 6. Funding

(Funding statement. If none, state "This work received no specific
funding.")

# 7. Competing interests

(Competing interests statement.)

# 8. Author contributions

Author contributions follow the CRediT taxonomy (see `_quarto.yml`).

# 9. Data availability

The protocol, search strategy, and data extraction form are deposited
on OSF at <OSF URL>. The full review data, R/Stata code, and
risk-of-bias assessments will be made available alongside the final
manuscript.

# References

::: bibliography
:::
